behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is CLI-observable output correctness against a real repo state. A reliable check needs a real git repo rename and end-to-end stdout readback (SYSTEM TRIAD #3). Integration was rejected because behavior text is about final report semantics from repository analysis, not only an internal handoff. Agentic/Workflow rejected by text: this is git/CLI logic with no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit file A, rename A->B in a later commit, run the CLI, and assert stdout report shows one rename and does not increment added/deleted for that rename.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff behavior depends on real git diff output/flags and CLI subprocess handling. Mocks can hide real binary-format edge cases. Needs real repo + binary file + CLI run with observable stdout result (SYSTEM TRIAD #3). Agentic/Workflow rejected by behavior text: no model call, only git/CLI processing.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary real git repo, add/modify a binary file and at least one text file, run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes binary-related file counts without crashing.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD behavior is tied to real git subprocess error-path semantics in an actual repo with zero commits. In-process fakes may not reproduce real exit/stderr behavior. Requires end-to-end CLI execution and output/exit observation (SYSTEM TRIAD #2 and #3). Agentic/Workflow rejected by text: no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit (or documented non-error exit), and assert stdout is an empty/zero-count report rather than an exception or fatal message.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real repository history, requiring real git history construction and round-trip verification of externally visible report counts (SYSTEM TRIAD #3). Integration was rejected because behavior explicitly references actual repository history, not just module contracts. Agentic/Workflow rejected by text: deterministic SCM analysis, no AI model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo with a controlled sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies, run the CLI over the target range, and assert reported category counts exactly match the known history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics rely on real git index/working-tree behavior, which fakes often miss. Requires real repo state transitions and CLI output equivalence check via stdout (SYSTEM TRIAD #3). Integration rejected because behavior text targets git state behavior at CLI boundary. Agentic/Workflow rejected by text: no AI/ML or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary real git repo, create a change set, capture CLI output when changes are unstaged, then stage the same changes and rerun; assert outputs are identical.
